steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: If $n$ spaces fill a 180-foot curb, each space has width $\frac{180}{n}$ feet. The standard width constraint gives $7.5 \le \frac{180}{n} \le 9.0$.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: From $\frac{180}{n} \le 9$: multiply both sides by $n$ to get $180 \le 9n$, so $n \ge 20$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: From $7.5 \le \frac{180}{n}$: multiply both sides by $n$ to get $7.5n \le 180$, so $n \le 24$.

eliminationHtml:
• Choice A — ‘$24 \le n \le 180$’: The upper bound 180 has no basis; a 180-foot curb cannot hold 180 spaces each at least 7.5 feet wide. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘$7.5 \le n \le 9$’: These are the widths in feet, not the number of spaces. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘$20 \le n \le 180$’: The lower bound 20 is correct, but the upper bound 180 is far too high. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘$20 \le n \le 24$’: $180 \div 9 = 20$ (minimum spaces) and $180 \div 7.5 = 24$ (maximum spaces). Both bounds are correct. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
